items.title: AI agents will soon be able to match human traders, Robinhood CEO tells CNBC
items.description: Vlad Tenev spoke about the potential of AI agents in trading in an interview with CNBC.

items.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
items.description: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
items.description: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

items.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine
items.description: In 2018, the European Commission slapped Google with the record-breaking penalty on the grounds that it abused Android's mobile dominance.

items.title: Nvidia offers start-up customers chance to swap compute power for revenue share
items.description: Nvidia says it will give start-ups the chance to swap access to compute power for a slice of future profits under a new program aimed at AI-focused firms.

items.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
items.description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

items.title: M&A is ‘on fire’ as large-cap firms simplify, Citi UK CEO says
items.description: Citi U.K. CEO Tiina Lee said U.K. M&A is “on fire” as companies simplify and overseas buyers target British assets.

items.title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
items.description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted more than 7% in early Thursday trading.

items.title: Apple plans five new iPhones through 2027, eyes Chinese-made chips amid foldable push, reports say
items.description: Apple is planning five new iPhones by 2027 and is considering Chinese memory suppliers as it expands foldable production, reports say.

items.title: Meta says WhatsApp usernames are safeguarded against scams after India flags cybersecurity risks
items.description: Meta has defended its WhatsApp username feature after the Indian government raised cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
items.description: The Russian defence ministry said that it launched a "massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones."

items.title: Volkswagen braces for boardroom showdown over historic cost-cutting plan
items.description: Volkswagen is poised for a boardroom showdown following reports that the auto giant is weighing up shutting four factories and implementing 100,000 job cuts.

items.title: Amazon is designing its own AI chips for Echo, Fire TV and future devices, exec tells CNBC
items.description: Amazon hardware chief Panos Panay says the company is designing custom chips for key devices as it experiments with AI gadgets.

items.title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
items.description: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

items.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
items.description: An historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

items.title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
items.description: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

items.title:
Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake up
India's delivery-in-minutes sector
items.description: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

items.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
items.description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

items.title: Why Japanese airlines' $400 fuel surcharges are hitting now — and when they will end
items.description: Fuel surcharges at Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways increased Wednesday, despite falling jet fuel costs in Asia.

items.title: Trump got $15,000 in FIFA tickets from Gianni Infantino. Next up: Presenting the World Cup trophy
items.description: Trump’s ties to FIFA and Gianni Infantino face new scrutiny as the World Cup enters the knockout round and European lawmakers press over a Peace Prize complaint.
